title: STANDARD FOR SPICES DERIVED FROM DRIED OR DEHYDRATED FRUITS AND BERRIES: ALLSPICE, JUNIPER BERRY AND STAR ANISE CXS 358-2024

sections.section: STANDARD FOR SPICES DERIVED FROM DRIED OR DEHYDRATED FRUITS AND BERRIES: ALLSPICE, JUNIPER BERRY AND STAR ANISE CXS 358-2024
sections.summary: Document title and adoption note for the Codex standard covering spices derived from dried/dehydrated fruits and berries (allspice, juniper berry, star anise), adopted in 2024.

Applies to spices from dried or dehydrated fruits and berries (defined in Section 2.1) offered for direct consumption, as food‑processing ingredients or for repackaging, excludes products for industrial processing, and allows species to be specified by contract.
Section heading introducing the product description, covering product definition (Table 1 species), permitted styles (whole, cut/broken, ground) and optional sizing provisions.
Defines the covered products as dried or dehydrated fruits and berries listed in Table 1: allspice (Pimenta dioica), juniper berry (Juniperus communis) and star anise (Illicium verum).
Permits styles of product to be whole, cut/broken or ground/powdered, and allows other distinct styles provided they are labelled accordingly.
Allows optional sizing of whole or cut products according to trade practice and contract, and requires the package to indicate the size designation and the sizing method when sized.
Introduces essential composition (Annex I) and quality factors, covering organoleptic criteria, optional classification/grade minimums and required chemical/physical characteristics (see Annex I).
Requires that the dried or dehydrated fruits and berries conform to the composition requirements set out in Annex I.

Specifies quality factors including: characteristic odour, flavour and colour free from foreign odours/flavours (notably rancidity and mustiness); that Annex I sets minimum requirements when products are classified/graded; and that chemical/physical requirements and allowed defects are detailed in Annex I tables.
Requires a characteristic odour, flavour and colour (which may vary with geoclimatic conditions) and absence of foreign odour, flavour or colour, especially rancidity and mustiness.
States that where products are traded as classified/graded, the provisions in Annex I apply as the minimum requirements for the lowest class/grade.

Requires compliance with Annex I chemical (Table A1) and physical (Table A2) characteristics, and that permitted defects must not adversely affect the product's general appearance, keeping quality or presentation in the package.
Anticaking agents listed in Table 3 of the Codex General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) are acceptable for use in powdered forms of foods that conform to this standard.
Processing aids used in products conforming to this standard should be consistent with the Codex Guidelines on substances used as processing aids (CXG 75-2010).

Products covered by this standard shall comply with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CXS 193-1995), the Code of Practice for prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices (CXC 78-2017), other relevant Codex texts, and Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Products should be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low‑Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015, Annex III) and other relevant Codex texts, and must comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Containers must be filled as fully as practicable without impairing quality and must be consistent with a proper declaration of contents for the product.

Products must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985). Specific provisions require the product name/style/trade name as described in Sections 2.1–2.2, mandatory declaration of country of origin (with country/region/year of harvest optional), commercial identification (style; class/grade if applicable; size if sized), and non‑retail container labelling consistent with CXS 346-2021.
The product name must follow Section 2.1, may indicate the style per Section 2.2, and may include trade name, variety or cultivar on the label.

sections.section: 8.2 Country of origin and country of harvest
sections.summary: Country of origin must be declared; country of harvest, region of harvest and year of harvest are optional.
Commercial identification should include the style, class/grade if applicable, and size if the product is sized (size is optional).
Labelling of non‑retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Section heading only; no substantive provisions are provided in this section.

Compliance with this standard must be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) that are relevant to the specific provisions.
Sampling plan: to be developed (no sampling plan provided).

Annex I contains Table A1 with product- and style-specific chemical characteristic limits (e.g., maximum moisture, total ash, acid‑insoluble ash and minimum volatile oil) for spices from dried/dehydrated fruits and berries, and Table A2 with physical/defect limits (e.g., dead whole insects, mammalian excreta, mould, insect infestation, extraneous/foreign matter, live insects) plus notes clarifying N/A entries and measurement rules.
